preamble: The shift from the voiceless bilabial fricative [ɸ] to the voiceless bilabial stop [p] is a process of fortition (strengthening). It involves narrowing the articulatory constriction until a full oral blockage is formed.

examples.note: Common when speakers of languages without bilabial fricatives adapt loanwords (e.g., in some varieties of English or Spanish adapting Japanese [ɸ]).

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
